rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. CARDOZA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PARENT, ALTER EGO, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Quintec Industries, Inc.¿ motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. CARDOZA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PARENT, ALTER EGO, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kelly Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to breach of implied warranty, fraud, civil conspiracy, enterprise liability, false representation and punitive damages and denied as to all other issues. Liberally construed, John Cardoza¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Elizabeth Cardoza was exposed to Paco Quik-Set joint compound. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable fact on breach of implied warranty, fraud, civil conspiracy, enterprise liability, false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR ESPOSTO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley and the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing due to the conduct of defendant. The objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley and the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE GALLASPIE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Goodman Lumber Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing joint compound purchased from defendant. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers. The objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Perini Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (contractor liability and negligence) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (strict liability). Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for strict liability. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Nibco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to original asbestos-containing internal components of Nibco valves. The objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
